name: Library
description: A bookshelf-lined reading room. Perimeter walls are clad with stacks of bookshelves (2-3 blocks tall) with gaps for the door and window. A central lectern serves as the focal reading surface; a 3-block reading table flanked by stair-block chairs sits to one side. Wall-mounted lanterns provide steady light. Fantasy variants include an enchanting table beside the lectern; medieval variants may include a ladder to an upper gallery when the room is tall enough.

required_furniture.rule: Line every interior wall except the door and window cells; stack 2-3 high.
At least one centered between the bookshelves; add a second when floor area >= 10x10.
Three blocks of @primary along the z axis, offset from the lectern column.
Two stair-block chairs flank the reading table; one in tight rooms.
Mount on the top interior corners of the walls, one row below the ceiling.
Fantasy style only — placed adjacent to the central lectern.
Vertical ladder against the east interior wall when AABB is multi-floor friendly (height >= 5 and footprint >= 8x8).

placement_rules: Bookshelves line every wall except door/window
Door opens on the south wall, window on the north wall
Lanterns mounted near the ceiling for ambient light without obstructing shelves
lighting_ventilation: Two lanterns (four in larger rooms) hang near the ceiling corners for warm reading light. A glass pane on the north wall provides daylight without UV damage to books; the south door admits cross-flow ventilation when open.

medieval.notes: Plain oak shelves, wool carpet optional, ladder to a mezzanine when room is tall.

modern.notes: Bookshelves remain vanilla oak (canonical block) against pale stone walls; redstone lamps replace lanterns at the style-pack level.

fantasy.notes: Adds an enchanting table beside the central lectern; sea lanterns substitute for lanterns at the style-pack level.
